title: Senecio gawlerensis
taxonomy: Senecio gawlerensis is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to Australia, specifically in the state of South Australia. The classification of Senecio gawlerensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Senecio, Species: S. gawlerensis. The genus Senecio is a large and diverse group of plants, commonly known as ragworts or groundels. The exact classification of Senecio gawlerensis may vary depending on the source and the taxonomic system used.

intro: Senecio gawlerensis, also known as Gawler's ragwort, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Senecio gawlerensis include sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to possess antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to relieve respiratory issues and promote general well-being.
